title: A002: Implement shared test infrastructure
summary: Review #4 feedback from gndrmnn addressed cleanly: all test files migrated from if/else + t.Errorf/t.Fatalf to gomega assertions (g := NewGomegaWithT(t) with dot import), and fake CRD YAMLs reorganized from flat directory into operator-specific subdirectories (cert-manager/, external-secrets/, mariadb-operator/, memcached-operator/, rabbitmq-operator/). The fakeCRDsDirs() function now dynamically discovers subdirectories. All 28 unit tests pass, go vet and golangci-lint are clean, no regressions introduced.

tests_checklist.item: Integration tests present and guarded by SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable
Edge cases covered (zero replicas, empty server list, not-found errors, idempotency)
Gomega assertions produce readable failure messages with context strings
Tests are independent (no shared mutable state between test functions)

code_quality_checklist.item: Gomega pattern applied consistently across all 5 test files
Dot import used uniformly for gomega
No dead code or unused imports after migration
fakeCRDsDirs() properly validates directory existence and non-empty
doc.go updated to reflect new subdirectory structure

security_checklist.item: No user-facing input surfaces (test infrastructure only)

architecture_checklist.item: CRD subdirectory grouping follows operator ownership boundaries
Dynamic subdirectory discovery avoids hardcoded paths
No new go.mod — testutil stays within internal/common module

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated test helpers across files
Changes scoped precisely to reviewer feedback (gomega + subdirs)

fail_fast_checklist.item: fakeCRDsDirs() panics on missing directory or empty subdirectories
Gomega assertions fail immediately on first mismatch

detected_patterns.name: Gomega test assertions with NewGomegaWithT
detected_patterns.applies_when: Writing unit or integration tests in any testutil package or consumer of testutil
detected_patterns.description: All test functions use gomega via dot import (`. "github.com/onsi/gomega"`) and create a local `g := NewGomegaWithT(t)` at the start of each test function. Assertions use `g.Expect(...)` with gomega matchers (To, NotTo, Succeed, HaveOccurred, BeTrue, Equal, HaveLen, HaveKeyWithValue, ContainSubstring, BeNil, BeEmpty, BeEquivalentTo, Panic). This replaces raw if/else + t.Errorf/t.Fatalf patterns.

examples.code:
func TestSimulateMariaDBReady(t *testing.T) {
	g := NewGomegaWithT(t)

	mariadb := newUnstructured("k8s.mariadb.com", "v1alpha1", "MariaDB", "test-mariadb", "default")
	c := fake.NewClientBuilder().
		WithScheme(newScheme()).
		WithObjects(mariadb).
		WithStatusSubresource(mariadb).
		Build()

	err := SimulateMariaDBReady(context.Background(), c, client.ObjectKeyFromObject(mariadb), 3)
	g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())

detected_patterns.name: Fake CRD subdirectory grouping by operator
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding new external operator CRDs for envtest integration tests
detected_patterns.description: Fake CRD YAML files are organized into subdirectories named after the external operator that owns them (e.g., mariadb-operator/, cert-manager/, external-secrets/). Each subdirectory contains only the CRDs belonging to that operator. The fakeCRDsDirs() function in setup.go dynamically discovers these subdirectories at runtime. Adding a new operator requires only creating a new subdirectory with its CRD YAMLs — no code changes needed.
